summary.intent: Fix gossamer's UI loading issue, ensure the poll loop picks up new local git commits on the checkpoints branch, and improve the checkpoint detail formatting in the Electron app's SessionDetail page.
summary.outcome: Fixed async git operations in the indexer, restored and combined npm start scripts, added a rich diff viewer using diff2html with full light/dark mode theming, and applied InlineMarkdown rendering to checkpoint text fields.

repo: Gossamer is an Electron app (electron-vite) with a separate Node.js HTTP/WebSocket server; both must run together — `npm start` now uses concurrently to launch both

The entire/checkpoints/v1 branch is written by the `entire` post-commit hook (`entire hooks git post-commit`); if push fails, entire reverts the local commit, so SSH agent must be configured correctly
Shadow branches (`entire/<hash>-<hash>`) hold in-progress sessions; checkpoints only appear on entire/checkpoints/v1 after session finalization and a successful hook run

The gossamer indexer uses a git worktree at .gossamer/checkpoints checked out from entire/checkpoints/v1; the poll loop must use refs/heads/ prefix to resolve the local branch, not the remote tracking ref
CheckpointIdGitOidJoin.gitOid is the @id field — findUnique must use gitOid, not checkpointId; use findFirst for checkpointId lookups
The app uses Mantine's light-dark() CSS function (not @media prefers-color-scheme) for theme switching; all custom CSS must follow this pattern to work correctly
diff2html colorScheme: 'auto' uses @media queries which conflict with Mantine's light-dark(); use colorScheme: 'light' and override everything with light-dark() in CSS
Mantine exposes var(--mantine-font-family-monospace) which gives the full ui-monospace stack used throughout the app

code.finding: execSync for git fetch was blocking the entire Node.js event loop; replaced with promisified exec with 10s timeout
runIndex poll loop: bare branch name 'entire/checkpoints/v1' in git reset --hard resolves ambiguously to origin/ tracking ref when both point to same SHA; must use refs/heads/ prefix

merge --ff-only in runIndex was dangerous — it fast-forwarded local branch to match origin, which pulled in a bad commit during testing; removed entirely, gossamer should never advance the local branch
Files changed section was hidden when both filesTouched and diffStats were empty; must always render when expanded and show loading state while fetching
diff2html's .d2h-file-list (summary row at top) and .d2h-tag (CHANGED badge) must be display:none to avoid duplicate filename display; use var(--mantine-font-family-monospace) for code font

workflow: When git subprocesses hang on SSH prompts in a Node.js server, the entire event loop blocks — always use async exec with timeout for git operations in servers
Setting GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT=0 and SSH BatchMode=yes only suppresses terminal prompts; macOS Keychain shows GUI dialogs that bypass both — the real fix is a properly configured SSH agent
Running git commit --allow-empty in a worktree with staged changes will include those changes — always check git status before creating test commits
diff2html's CSS custom property approach (--d2h-*) doesn't win against its own :root declarations without !important; scoped class + light-dark() is the correct override strategy

friction: Multiple rounds of SSH suppression (GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT, BatchMode, SSH_AUTH_SOCK clearing, --no-track) all failed before reverting to 'trust the user's SSH agent' — significant wasted effort
Accidental destructive git commit: testing push behavior staged pending deletions from a failed hook run and pushed them to origin, requiring a force-push recovery
duplicate GIT_LOCAL_ENV declaration in server.ts from iterative SSH suppression attempts caused a scope error
diff2html dark mode theming required three full rewrites of the CSS (CSS variables, @media, then light-dark()) before finding the correct approach
entire post-commit hook uses 2>/dev/null || true, silently swallowing errors that caused checkpoints to never appear — very hard to diagnose
open_items: filesTouched is empty in the DB for checkpoint d2014fd45299 and possibly others — investigate whether metadata.json has files_touched field and whether the indexer reads it correctly
CheckpointIdGitOidJoin may not have a gitOid mapping for older checkpoints, causing the diff endpoint to return [] — no fallback beyond filesTouched filename list
The entire post-commit hook's silent failure mode (2>/dev/null || true) should be investigated — errors during checkpoint creation are invisible and hard to debug
Checkpoint 18f992950c10 (from the first commit of session 7d0ed455) was lost because the hook silently failed on that commit; no retroactive recovery possible
The diff endpoint runs git show on every expand — consider caching the response on the server side for repeated opens of the same checkpoint
